message:
refactor(channel,net,sync): reduce lock scope, fix unused bindings, and eliminate heap allocations in tests

A sweep of code-quality improvements across multiple modules, all targeting
test code and Drop-adjacent paths. No behavioral changes to production code.

channel/mpsc.rs — Lock scope reduction in audit tests:
- Extracted values from MutexGuard before running assertions, preventing
  the lock from being held during assertion formatting/panic machinery
- Renamed _permit → permit to silence unused-binding warnings and clarify
  intent (the permit IS used — it's aborted two lines later)

channel/oneshot.rs — Test reorganization for clarity:
- Moved CountWaker struct definition above the test body in
  sender_drop_wakes_pending_receiver so the type is visible before its
  first use
- Renamed waker_state/waker_state2 → notify_count (clearer semantics)
- Renamed waker → poll_waker to distinguish from other waker concepts
- Inlined Arc::clone into Waker::from call to reduce intermediate bindings

net/websocket/frame.rs — Heap allocation elimination + unused result fixes:
- Replaced vec![0u8; N] with [0u8; N] stack arrays in 3 test decode paths
  (100-byte and 200-byte payloads are well within stack budget)
- Added let _ = to 3 Frame::close() calls in #[should_panic] tests to
  suppress unused-result warnings without affecting panic behavior

sync/rwlock.rs — Unused binding fixes:
- Renamed _write_guard → write_guard (it's explicitly dropped 8 lines later,
  so the underscore prefix is misleading about its intentional use)
- Renamed cx → _cx in owned guard tests where the context is required by
  the API but not referenced in the test body

sync/semaphore.rs — Same pattern:
- Renamed _held → held (explicitly dropped later to release the permit,
  not an unused binding)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
